metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A constructborn city where Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct, Alchemy Lab Beacon, Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Bastion Zero should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Bastion Zero organizes power through Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep, public response through Alchemy Lab Beacon, civic memory through Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct, and daily life through S1 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, S2 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S3 Public Works Authority Hall, S4 University / Lyceum Civic Hall, S5 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Stone, Ceramic Mosaic, Steel, Arcane Glow, Copper, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Construct Resonance, and exact anchors including Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep, Alchemy Lab Beacon, Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct, Star Observatory, Border Wall, Lecture Hall, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A constructborn civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct, Alchemy Lab Beacon, Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep, and section anchors such as Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Public Works Authority Hall, University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Bastion Zero uses city code BZO.
The Wonder is Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct.
The Beacon is Alchemy Lab Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, S2 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S3 Public Works Authority Hall, S4 University / Lyceum Civic Hall, S5 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Bastion Zero as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Bastion Zero's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

forms: Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct
skyline: Living Gear Bastion of the First Construct
infrastructure: records, permits, markets, services, or rituals implied by anchors
colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as constructborn and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as none explicitly dominant in the current anchors.

speechPatterns: public notices are tied to section anchors
local speech distinguishes civic authority, ordinary errands, and landmark obligations

streets.appearance: Streets should show Bastion Zero's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, S2 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S3 Public Works Authority Hall, S4 University / Lyceum Civic Hall, S5 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Clockwork Reliefs Iron Mandate Keep, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
